verses.text: 'Stand fast... in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free'; be not 'entangled again with the yoke of bondage.' Hold liberty. Do not return to the law's yoke.
'Stand fast... in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free'; be not 'entangled again with the yoke of bondage.' Hold liberty. Do not return to the law's yoke.
'Stand fast... in the liberty wherewith Christ hath made us free'; be not 'entangled again with the yoke of bondage.' Hold liberty. Do not return to the law's yoke.
In Christ neither circumcision avails, 'but faith which worketh by love.' Working faith. True faith shows itself in love.
In Christ neither circumcision avails, 'but faith which worketh by love.' Working faith. True faith shows itself in love.
In Christ neither circumcision avails, 'but faith which worketh by love.' Working faith. True faith shows itself in love.
Ye did run well; who hindered you? 'a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.' Beware. A little error spreads through all.
Ye did run well; who hindered you? 'a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.' Beware. A little error spreads through all.
Ye did run well; who hindered you? 'a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.' Beware. A little error spreads through all.
He that troubles you shall bear his judgment; 'the offence of the cross' has ceased among the compromisers. Faithfulness. The cross always offends the legalist.
He that troubles you shall bear his judgment; 'the offence of the cross' has ceased among the compromisers. Faithfulness. The cross always offends the legalist.
He that troubles you shall bear his judgment; 'the offence of the cross' has ceased among the compromisers. Faithfulness. The cross always offends the legalist.
Ye are called to liberty; 'by love serve one another'; love thy neighbour as thyself. Loving service. Freedom is for serving, not self-indulgence.
Ye are called to liberty; 'by love serve one another'; love thy neighbour as thyself. Loving service. Freedom is for serving, not self-indulgence.
Ye are called to liberty; 'by love serve one another'; love thy neighbour as thyself. Loving service. Freedom is for serving, not self-indulgence.
'Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh'; the two are contrary. Inner conflict. Spirit and flesh war within.
'Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh'; the two are contrary. Inner conflict. Spirit and flesh war within.
'Walk in the Spirit, and ye shall not fulfil the lust of the flesh'; the two are contrary. Inner conflict. Spirit and flesh war within.
The works of the flesh are manifest — adultery, hatred, envyings; such 'shall not inherit the kingdom.' Plain sins. The fleshly life shuts out the kingdom.
The works of the flesh are manifest — adultery, hatred, envyings; such 'shall not inherit the kingdom.' Plain sins. The fleshly life shuts out the kingdom.
The works of the flesh are manifest — adultery, hatred, envyings; such 'shall not inherit the kingdom.' Plain sins. The fleshly life shuts out the kingdom.
'The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance.' Spirit's fruit. These graces grow where the Spirit reigns.
'The fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, longsuffering, gentleness, goodness, faith, meekness, temperance.' Spirit's fruit. These graces grow where the Spirit reigns.
They that are Christ's 'have crucified the flesh'; let us walk in the Spirit. Crucified flesh. The believer puts to death his old desires.
